title: switch statements should include default clauses
description: "switch" statements should have "default" clausesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEcwetype-dependentWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe requirement for a finaldefaultclause is defensive programming. The clause should either take appropriate action, or contain a suitable comment as to why no action is taken.Noncompliant code exampleswitch (param) { //missing default clause case 0: doSomething(); break; case 1: doSomethingElse(); break; } switch (param) { default: // default clause should be the last one error(); break; case 0: doSomething(); break; case 1: doSomethingElse(); break; }Compliant solutionswitch (param) { case 0: doSomething(); break; case 1: doSomethingElse(); break; default: error(); break; }Exc
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Switch statement is missing a default clause. Add a default case for defensive programming.
Default clause should be the last clause in switch statement.
